status.next_allowed_action: Run git log --oneline -15 and npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine before implementing work unit 3.1.

execution_rules.branch_base: Reviewed base commit is f3a7ccd on stage-4. Branch from that commit, or update plan/progress with a newly reviewed base commit before implementation starts.
execution_rules.commit_policy: Commit each verified work unit and record the hash. In this phased plan, each numbered task inside a phase is a work unit unless the plan explicitly says otherwise.
execution_rules.scope_policy: Complete stage 4.5 outcome; do not reduce to shortcut delivery. Reduce accidental implementation complexity by reusing existing host shells first.
execution_rules.acceptance_correction_policy: If implementation discovers a wrong or impossible acceptance criterion, stop, record the blocker, update the plan/progress through a review note, and do not keep implementing stale criteria.
execution_rules.browser_verification_policy: Add repeatable browser regression scripts for workbench and offline graph interactions. Codex in-app Browser may be used for supplemental screenshots and observations in Codex runs, but it is not the sole portable verification path because Claude Code and CI may not expose it.

decision_log.reason: The stage spans graph-engine, workbench UI, offline HTML, and regression/browser verification.
rejected_alternatives: Direct execution
Flat checklist

decision_log.decision: Reuse existing graph-engine helpers for viewport, search, minimap, and markdown cleanup.
decision_log.reason: The helpers already exist and match the design requirements.
rejected_alternatives: New viewport math
New search implementation
Ad hoc summary extraction

decision_log.decision: Use the workbench right drawer for graph reading and selection.
decision_log.reason: The design cancels the floating selection panel and assigns reading/selection to the drawer.
rejected_alternatives: Keep the floating selection panel
Open a separate graph-specific drawer outside RightDrawer

decision_log.decision: Keep the full stage 4.5 scope with a complexity guard.
decision_log.reason: The author explicitly chose complete delivery over a shortcut reduction.
rejected_alternatives: Shrink to a minimal graph fix
Defer user-visible graph usability goals to follow-up work

decision_log.decision: Offline HTML must include a complete non-AI selection panel.
decision_log.reason: Exported graphs need visible selected facts, even though they cannot offer chat actions.
rejected_alternatives: Only highlight offline selections
Omit offline selection facts because ask actions are unavailable

decision_log.decision: Separate graph selection-change callbacks from ask actions.
decision_log.reason: Selection is a graph state transition; asking AI is a later drawer command initiated by the user.
rejected_alternatives: Continue using onAsk as the selection notification name
Document the naming mismatch without changing the boundary

decision_log.decision: Pass page path plus node metadata when opening the graph reader.
decision_log.reason: The reader needs title, type, source/date metadata, and action availability; markdown body parsing would be brittle.
rejected_alternatives: Pass only a path
Infer graph reader metadata from markdown body

decision_log.decision: Add explicit right-drawer graph reader and graph selection modes.
decision_log.reason: Generic wiki preview state cannot safely carry graph metadata, selection, and close/clear behavior.
rejected_alternatives: Reuse the generic wiki preview mode with extra flags
Mix graph selection state into the existing wiki drawer state

decision_log.decision: Add a public graph clear-interaction method.
decision_log.reason: Drawer close, Esc, knowledge-base switch, and graph view switch must clear highlights and selections through one tested path.
rejected_alternatives: Rely on indirect focus or selection side effects
Clear React drawer state without clearing engine state

decision_log.decision: Split graph reader and graph selection into dedicated components.
decision_log.reason: GraphPanel and RightDrawer are shell components; embedding full graph drawer content there would make the complete stage hard to test and maintain.
rejected_alternatives: Keep the split optional
Inline graph reader and graph selection UI inside existing shell components

decision_log.decision: Split graph-engine renderer internals by feature.
decision_log.reason: static-renderer.ts is already over 1000 lines and stage 4.5 adds several independent UI subsystems.
rejected_alternatives: Keep adding viewport, search, legend, reader, preview, and styles inline
Rely only on comments inside a large renderer file

decision_log.decision: Refactor workbench drawer state into one discriminated object.
decision_log.reason: Scattered drawer fields can create contradictory mode, content, loading, and selection combinations once graph reader and graph selection are added.
rejected_alternatives: Keep adding fields to the existing independent drawer state variables
Rely on careful manual resets between drawer modes

decision_log.decision: Move workbench graph styles into graph.css.
decision_log.reason: index.css is already large and graph 4.5 adds enough dedicated styling to deserve a focused file.
rejected_alternatives: Continue adding all graph styles to index.css
Mix graph drawer styles with generic drawer styles

decision_log.decision: Require repeatable browser interaction tests plus Codex Browser supplemental evidence.
decision_log.reason: Core graph behavior crosses engine, React drawer, generated HTML, keyboard, and pointer interactions; scripts make it repeatable while Codex Browser gives live visual evidence in Codex runs.
rejected_alternatives: Treat browser observations as enough without a committed script
Depend only on Codex Browser even though Claude Code and CI may not expose it

decision_log.reason: Smoothness can regress even when DOM string checks and unit tests pass.
rejected_alternatives: Rely on subjective feel checks only
Wait until performance regressions are reported by users

decision_log.decision: Absorb outside-voice hardening without reducing scope.
decision_log.reason: Codex outside voice found underspecified focus, Esc, pointer, fixture, cache, security, branch, and commit-boundary details; these are implementation guardrails, not product scope cuts.
rejected_alternatives: Leave the plan ambiguous and rely on executor judgment
Use outside voice as a reason to shrink the stage

phases.title: Smooth Viewport Navigation
phases.goal: Make zoom, pan, fit, minimap viewport, and zoom-linked density work in both hosts.
acceptance: Browser check at 1440 x 960 proves wheel zoom centers on pointer, blank drag pans, and blank double-click fits.
Dense offline HTML check records 3 seconds of continuous wheel interaction without visual freeze; frame-rate evidence is recorded when tooling supports it.
evidence: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 125 tests passing.

bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0 and captured workbench/docs/stage-4.5-artifacts/stage-4.5-offline-navigation.png for 1440 x 960 wheel zoom, blank drag pan, and blank double-click fit.

Dense offline HTML check ran 3 seconds of continuous wheel interaction at 1440 x 960, recorded 179 frames over 3009ms (59.5fps), changed the viewport transform, and captured workbench/docs/stage-4.5-artifacts/stage-4.5-offline-dense-wheel.png plus stage-4.5-offline-dense-wheel.json.

tasks.title: Introduce renderer viewport state and one transform layer.
verification: RED: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine failed because ../src/render did not export rendererViewportToTransform.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 118 tests passing, including renderer viewport state tests.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.

tasks.title: Wire wheel zoom, blank drag pan, and blank double-click fit.
verification: RED: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine failed because createViewportFrameCommitter and related viewport navigation helpers were not exported.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 122 tests passing, including wheel delta, pointer-centered zoom, pan, fit, and rAF coalescing checks.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0 and captured workbench/docs/stage-4.5-artifacts/stage-4.5-offline-navigation.png.

tasks.title: Add minimap viewport rectangle and animated jumps.
verification: RED: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine failed because rendererViewportToMinimapRect was not exported or implemented yet.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 123 tests passing, including minimap viewport rectangle mapping.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.
bash tests/graph-html-minimap.regression-1.sh exited 0 and asserted minimap viewport markup plus runtime update hooks.

tasks.title: Upgrade density to use screen-effective density.
verification: RED: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine failed because ../src/render did not export nodeDisplayModeForDensity.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 125 tests passing, including screen-effective density tests.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.
bash tests/graph-html-density.regression-1.sh exited 0 and proved card, compact, and point transitions are reachable through viewport scale.

phases.title: Reading Drawer And Selection Semantics
phases.goal: Make node click read, make selection an upgraded state, and fix single-node action mapping.
acceptance: Browser checks at 1440 x 960 and 390 x 844 prove node click opens reader, Shift-click opens selection, Esc clears, and no selection panel overlays the canvas.
Browser check proves reader state has no learning queue, no summary block, no confidence list, and no neighbor section.
evidence: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 128 tests passing.
node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/*.test.ts exited 0 with 9 tests passing.

bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target workbench exited 0 and now covers 1440 x 960 plus 390 x 844: plain node click opens the right-drawer reader, Shift-click opens the right-drawer selection, Esc closes and clears graph highlights, and .graph-selection-panel is absent.

Workbench browser regression proves the graph reader has no learning queue, summary block, or neighbor section, and proves single-node selection hides structural stats and omits 探索潜在联系 while multi-node selection shows Shift+点击 增删节点 and structural facts.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0, preserving offline reader, Shift selection, Esc clear, viewport, and dense interaction coverage.

tasks.title: Reproduce and pin down Shift multi-select behavior.
verification: Repro path: generated current offline graph HTML, opened it in Chromium at 1440 x 960, held Shift, clicked the first node, then clicked the second node.
Observed failure before the fix: selected node count changed from 1 after the first Shift-click to 0 after the second Shift-click.

Root cause: drag capture and event target were not the issue; selection kind nodes reached the renderer, but buildRenderableGraph only marked a single selectedNodeId and ignored multi-node selection for node highlights.
Regression assertion: packages/graph-engine/test/render-model.test.ts now asserts shift-style multi-node selections mark both nodes and minimap entries selected.
Regression assertion: tests/browser/graph-stage-4-5.mjs now asserts two Shift-clicked nodes remain aria-pressed in the graph.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 126 tests passing.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0.

tasks.title: Fix selection action mapping.
verification: RED: packages/graph-engine/test/select.test.ts now covers all six stage 4.5 action-table scenarios, including linked single page, isolated single page, single community, two-community neighbor selection, linked manual multi-select, and unlinked multi-select.
GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 128 tests passing.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.

Selection action mapping now gives single nodes 总结这一页 / 它和谁有关 / 在对话中引用, isolated single nodes also get 把它链入知识库, single nodes never get 探索潜在联系, and linked manual multi-select gets 总结这一组 / 探索它们的关系 instead of community-cluster actions.

tasks.title: Change graph click routing.
verification: GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 128 tests passing.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0.
node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/*.test.ts exited 0 with 4 tests passing.
npm run build -w @llm-wiki-agent/web exited 0.

bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0 and now proves plain node click opens the internal reader/highlight, Shift-click opens selection highlights, Esc clears selection/reader highlight, and clear interaction keeps the viewport transform.
Root cause for failed Esc browser check was keyboard focus outside the graph root; Esc handling now listens at the document boundary only while graph interaction state exists.

tasks.title: Move workbench reading into the right drawer.
verification: GREEN: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 128 tests passing before work and again after implementation.
node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/*.test.ts exited 0 with 7 tests passing, including drawer-state and graph-reader coverage.
npm run build -w @llm-wiki-agent/web exited 0.

bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target workbench exited 0 and captured workbench/docs/stage-4.5-artifacts/stage-4.5-workbench-reader.png, proving node click opens the right-drawer reader, the reader has no learning queue, summary block, or neighbor section, and a drawer wikilink click navigates to wiki/entities/B.md while keeping one graph node highlighted.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0 after preserving existing offline browser checks.

Root cause for the initial workbench reader regression failure: GraphReader passed onOpenPage as onWikiLinkSeen, so rendering A auto-opened B when the markdown wikilink was discovered; the callbacks are now split so discovery only focuses the graph and clicking opens the linked page.

tasks.title: Move workbench selection into the right drawer.
verification: RED: node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/graph-selection-drawer.test.ts failed because ../src/lib/graph-selection-drawer did not exist yet.
GREEN: node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/graph-selection-drawer.test.ts exited 0 with 2 tests passing for single-node and multi-node drawer rules.
node --import tsx --test workbench/web/test/*.test.ts exited 0 with 9 tests passing.
npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki-agent/web exited 0 after the selection drawer wiring changes.
npm run build -w @llm-wiki-agent/web exited 0.
npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 with 128 tests passing.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target workbench exited 0 and proves the selection UI is in the right drawer at 1440 x 960 and 390 x 844, not as the old canvas overlay.
bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline exited 0.

Root cause for missing workbench selection actions during verification: graph-engine only includes ask actions when the host declares onAsk capability; GraphPanel now declares that capability while the actual ask is still initiated by the right-drawer buttons.

phases.title: Search And Community Legend
phases.goal: Restore graph search and add a community legend shared by workbench and offline HTML.
acceptance: Browser checks at 1440 x 960 and 768 x 1024 prove search and legend work in workbench and offline HTML.

tasks.title: Add graph-scoped search UI.
Add result cycling and viewport focus.
Add community legend.
Keep host behavior consistent.

phases.title: Slim Nodes And Hover Preview
phases.goal: Reduce default node noise while preserving useful preview information on hover.
acceptance: Browser check on dense fixture proves preview appears for card, compact, and point nodes.
Browser checks at 1440 x 960 and 390 x 844 prove text does not overflow node cards or preview cards.

tasks.title: Slim default node DOM and CSS.
Add hover preview card.
Preserve dense-mode behavior.

phases.title: Integration, Offline HTML, Themes, And Release Docs
phases.goal: Prove the complete graph experience across workbench, offline HTML, themes, and viewports.
acceptance: Browser verification evidence is recorded for all named viewports and both themes.

tasks.title: Verify offline HTML capabilities and reader meta.
Verify workbench theme and responsive behavior.
Run full project verification.
Update docs and release notes if code changed behavior.

turn_log.entry: Plan and progress ledger created from workbench/docs/stage-4.5-design.md. No implementation work started.
Plan engineering review recorded D11 repeatable browser interaction tests, Codex Browser supplemental evidence, and dense graph performance contracts. No implementation work started.

Codex outside voice review completed and hardening items were folded into the plan: fixed base commit, work-unit commit boundaries, browser runner setup, Esc/focus/click-vs-drag contracts, cache invalidation, fixture-first coverage, and markdown sanitization. No implementation work started.

Commit policy aligned with spec-to-goal-plan: commit each verified work unit, where each numbered phase task is a work unit unless the plan explicitly says otherwise. /goal starter updated for direct use in a new session.

Work unit 1.1 implemented renderer viewport state and a single graph content transform layer. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine and npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine.

Work unit 1.2 wired wheel zoom, blank drag pan, blank double-click fit, and rAF-coalesced viewport writes. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, and bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline.

Work unit 1.3 added the minimap viewport rectangle and fit-jump animation state. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, and bash tests/graph-html-minimap.regression-1.sh.

Work unit 1.4 upgraded density to use viewport-scale-aware effective density without changing graph coordinates. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, and bash tests/graph-html-density.regression-1.sh.

Phase 1 acceptance passed. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, bash tests/graph-html-minimap.regression-1.sh, bash tests/graph-html-density.regression-1.sh, and bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline. Dense wheel evidence recorded 179 frames over 3009ms, 59.5fps.

Work unit 2.1 reproduced Shift multi-select in Chromium: first Shift-click selected one node, second Shift-click cleared graph highlights. Root cause was renderer selection state ignoring selection kind nodes, not drag capture or event target. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine, and bash tests/graph-browser-stage-4-5.regression-1.sh --target offline.
Work unit 2.2 fixed graph selection action mapping for all six design scenarios. Verification passed: npm run test -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine and npm run typecheck -w @llm-wiki/graph-engine.

Work unit 2.3 split graph read and selection routing. Plain node click emits open-page metadata and highlights the node; Shift/community selection emits selection-change separately; clearInteraction/Esc clears graph-local interaction without resetting viewport. Verification passed: graph-engine tests/typecheck, workbench web tests, web build, and offline browser regression.
